info.title: Fastly API
info.description:
Via the Fastly API you can perform any of the operations that are possible within the management console, including creating services, domains, and backends; and configuring rules or uploading your own application code, as well as perform account operations such as user administration and billing reports.

The API is organized into collections of endpoints that allow manipulation of objects related to Fastly services and accounts.

For the most accurate and up-to-date API reference content, visit [developer.fastly.com/reference/api/](https://developer.fastly.com/reference/api/).

get.summary: Retrieve service-level usage metrics for services with non-zero usage units
get.description:
"Returns product usage, broken down by service.
> **Authorization**: [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens) with at least [Billing](/guides/account-info/user-access-and-control/configuring-user-roles-and-permissions\") permissions.
"

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor value from the next_cursor field of a previous response, used to retrieve the next page. To request the first page, this should be empty.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results per page. The maximum is 10000.

parameters.name: product_id
parameters.description: The product identifier for the metrics returned (e.g., cdn_usage). This should be used along with usage_type_name.

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: The service identifier for the metrics being requested.

parameters.name: usage_type_name
parameters.description: The usage type name for the metrics returned (e.g., North America Requests). This should be used along with product_id.

get.description:
"Returns monthly usage metrics for customer by product.
> **Authorization**: [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens) with at least [Billing](/guides/account-info/user-access-and-control/configuring-user-roles-and-permissions\") permissions.
"

200.description: 200 A response containing usage metric elements.

"List all events for a particular customer. Events can be filtered by user, customer and event type. Events can be sorted by date.

parameters.description: Return events after a date and time in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: filter[created_at][gte]
parameters.description: Return events on and after a date and time in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: filter[created_at][lt]
parameters.description: Return events before a date and time in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: filter[created_at][lte]
parameters.description: Return events on and before a date and time in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: filter[created_at]
parameters.description: "Limit the returned events to a specific time frame. Accepts sub-parameters: lt, lte, gt, gte (e.g., filter[created_at][gt]=2022-01-12)."

parameters.name: filter[customer_id]
parameters.description: Limit the results returned to a specific customer.

parameters.name: filter[event_type]
parameters.description: Limit the returned events to a specific event_type.

parameters.name: filter[service_id]
parameters.description: Limit the results returned to a specific service.

parameters.name: filter[token_id]
parameters.description: Limit the returned events to a specific token.

parameters.name: filter[user_id]
parameters.description: Limit the results returned to a specific user.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The order in which to list the results by creation date.

get.description:
"The Suggest method performs a real-time query of the search term(s) against the known zone database,
making recommendations, stemming, and applying Unicode folding, IDN normalization, registrar supported-zone
restrictions, and other refinements. Note: Suggest method responses do not include domain availability status.
> **Authorization**: Requires [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens).
"

parameters.name: defaults
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of default zones to include in the search results response.

parameters.name: keywords
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of keywords for seeding the results. For example, a new gTLD like kitchen,
or a related keyword like vegan. Helpful for search result relevance (e.g. from a targeted ad click,
a user profile, etc.).

parameters.name: location
parameters.description:
Override IP location detection for country-code zones, with a two-character country code.
E.g. to include Geo-relevant zones in the search results.

parameters.name: vendor
parameters.description: The domain name of a specific registrar or vendor, for filtering results by the zones supported by the vendor.

get.description:
"The Status method checks the availability status of a single domain name.
> **Authorization**: Requires [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens).
"

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Include scope=estimate to perform an Estimated status check. Otherwise, a Precise status check is performed by default.

get.description:
"List all domains.
> **Authorization**: [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens) with at least [Engineer](/guides/account-info/user-access-and-control/configuring-user-roles-and-permissions\") permissions.
"

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor value from the next_cursor field of a previous response, used to retrieve the next page. To request the first page, this should be empty.

parameters.name: fqdn_match
parameters.description: (Optional) Filter fully-qualified domain name (FQDN) specifically by match type. If used, requires filtering by FQDN.

parameters.name: fqdn
parameters.description: (Optional) Filters results by the fully-qualified domain name. The type of match will be determined by the fqdn_match parameter.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit how many results are returned.

parameters.name: service_id
parameters.description: Filter results based on a service_id.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The order in which to list the results.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor value from the next_cursor field of a previous response, used to retrieve the next page. To request the first page, this should be empty.

parameters.name: datacenter
parameters.description: Limit query to one or more specific POPs. Values should be comma-separated.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Limit query to one or more specific domains. Values should be comma-separated.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: A valid ISO-8601-formatted date and time, or UNIX timestamp, indicating the exclusive end of the query time range. If not provided, a default is chosen based on the provided downsample value.

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description:
Dimensions to return in the query. Multiple dimensions may be separated by commas.
For example, group_by=domain will return one timeseries for every domain, as a total across all datacenters (POPs).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results per page. The maximum is 200.

parameters.name: metric
parameters.description: The metrics to retrieve. Multiple values should be comma-separated.

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: Limit query to one or more specific geographic regions. Values should be comma-separated.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: A valid ISO-8601-formatted date and time, or UNIX timestamp, indicating the inclusive start of the query time range. If not provided, a default is chosen based on the provided downsample value.

get.summary: Get real-time domain data from a specified time
get.description:
"Get real-time domain data for the specified reporting period. Specify 0 to get a single entry for the last complete second. The Timestamp field included in the response provides the time index of the latest entry in the dataset and can be provided as the start_timestamp of the next request for a seamless continuation of the dataset from one request to the next.
Due to processing latency, the earliest entry in the response dataset may be earlier than start_timestamp by the value of AggregateDelay.
> **Authorization**: Requires [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens).
"

get.summary: Get real-time domain data for the last 120 seconds

get.summary: Get a limited number of real-time domain data entries

"Get data for the max_entries seconds preceding the latest timestamp available for a service, up to a maximum of 120 entries.
> **Authorization**: Requires [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens).
"

parameters.name: max_entries
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to show.

get.description:
"Fetches historical stats for each of your Fastly services and groups the results by service ID.

"

parameters.name: by
parameters.description:
Duration of sample windows. One of:

hour - Group data by hour.
minute - Group data by minute.
day - Group data by day.

parameters.name: datacenter
parameters.description:
Limit query to one or more Fastly POPs. For multiple POPs use a comma-seperated list of POPs.
These must be all uppercase and be valid codes. A full list of POPs can be obtained from the
POPs endpoint.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Timestamp that defines the start of the window for which to fetch statistics, including the timestamp itself. Accepts Unix timestamps, or any form of input parsable by the Chronic Ruby library, such as 'yesterday', or 'two weeks ago'. Default varies based on the value of by.

parameters.name: region
parameters.description:
Limit query to a specific geographic region. One of:

usa - North America.
europe - Europe.
anzac - Australia and New Zealand.
asia - Asia.
asia_india - India.
asia_southkorea - South Korea.
africa_std - Africa.
mexico - Mexico.
southamerica_std - South America.

parameters.name: services
parameters.description: Limit the query to only the specified, comma-separated list of services.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Timestamp that defines the end of the window for which to fetch statistics. Accepts the same formats as from.

parameters.name: domain_exact_match
parameters.description: Determines whether the value in the domain parameter should be treated as a suffix or exact match. When false for suffix match, any request domain ending in the specified value will be considered a match (e.g., foo.com will match a.foo.com and b.foo.com in addition to foo.com). Defaults to true for exact match.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Limits data to the specified request domain. Defaults to all request domains.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Specifies the exclusive end time of the logs to be queried. Must be in valid RFC 3339 format.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of rows to return. Defaults to 10, limited to 100.

parameters.name: pops
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of Fastly POP codes. Omission defaults to all POPs.

parameters.name: service_id
parameters.description: Specifies the ID of the service for which data should be returned.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Specifies the inclusive start time of the logs to be queried. Must be in valid RFC 3339 format.

parameters.name: visualization
parameters.description: Specifies the Log Insights visualization for which data should be returned.

200.description: 200 Retrieves log insights for the specified service and visualization.

get.description:
"Retrieves aggregated log results.
> **Authorization**: [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens) with at least [Engineer](/guides/account-info/user-access-and-control/configuring-user-roles-and-permissions\") permissions.
"

parameters.name: dimensions
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields for grouping dimensions.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Specifies the exclusive end time of the logs to be queried. Must be in valid RFC3339 format.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Filter parameters take the form filter[ ][ ]= .
For example, filter[response_time][gte]=0.5 would filter for logs where the
response time was greater than or equal to 0.5 seconds. Multiple filter
expressions can be combined. The filters will be combined with a logical AND
operator so that the logs returned match all supplied filters.
Supported filter fields include domain, request_path, fastly_pop,
response_time, response_status, is_shield, is_edge,
client_os_name, client_device_type, client_browser_name, and
is_cache_hit.
Supported filter operators include = (applicable to string, numeric, and
boolean values), ends-with (applicable to string values), in (applicable
to lists of string and numeric values), not_in (applicable to lists of
string and numeric values), and gt, gte, lt, lte (applicable to
numeric values).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of rows to return. Defaults to 10, limited to 100.

parameters.name: series
parameters.description:
A data series to be computed for each grouping dimension. Series
definitions take the form [ ]. For example, series=avg[response_time]
will compute the average response time for each dimension value. At least one
series is required.

parameters.name: service_id
parameters.description: Specifies the ID of the service for which data should be returned.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of the series names whose values should be used
to sort the results. Prepend - to a series name to sort according to the
descending order of its values.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Specifies the source of the data for the aggregations.  Must be logs to retrieve log data.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Specifies the inclusive start time of the logs to be queried. Must be in valid RFC3339 format.

get.summary: Retrieve sustainability data as a time series
get.description:
"Retrieves sustainability data as a time series.
> **Authorization**: [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens) with at least [Engineer](/guides/account-info/user-access-and-control/configuring-user-roles-and-permissions\") permissions.
"

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor value from the next_cursor field of a previous response, used to retrieve the next page. To request the first page, this should be empty.

parameters.name: dimensions
parameters.description: Comma separated list of grouping dimensions. No grouping dimensions implies aggregation by time only.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Filter parameters are query string components that let you narrow down API responses by
including only records that match specific criteria.  Filter parameters take the form
filter[ ][ ]= or filter[ ]= .
For example, filter[country][in]=US,CA would filter for sustainability data
from usage of Fastly PoPs located in the United States or Canada. Multiple filter
expressions can be combined. The filters will be combined with a logical AND
operator so that the data returned match all supplied filters.
Supported filter operator in applies to lists of string values.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Specifies the inclusive start time of the data to be queried. Must be in valid RFC 3339 format.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: The duration of each interval in the time series.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return.

parameters.name: series
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of the data series to be computed for each time bucket and combination
of dimensions. Series definitions take the form [ ]. For sustainability,
the only function supported is sum. For example, series=sum[bandwidth_gb] will return
the sum of bandwidth for each dimensioned time bucket. At least one series is required.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Specifies the source of the data for the time series. Must be sustainability to retrieve sustainability data.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Specifies the exclusive end time of the data to be queried. Must be in valid RFC 3339 format.

200.description: 200 Retrieves time series results based on sustainability data.

put.summary: Create or recreate a secret in a store
put.description:
"Create or recreate a secret based on the secret's name.
The response object's recreated field will be true if the secret was recreated.
The secret field must be Base64-encoded because a secret can contain binary data.
In the example below, the unencoded secret is \"Hello, world!\".
> **Authorization**: [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens) with at least [Engineer](/guides/account-info/user-access-and-control/configuring-user-roles-and-permissions\") permissions.
"

post.summary: Create a new secret in a store
post.description:
"Create a new secret in a store.
Returns an error if a secret already exists with the same name.
See PUT and PATCH methods for ways to recreate an existing secret.
The secret field must be Base64-encoded because a secret can contain binary data.
In the example below, the unencoded secret is \"Hello, world!\".
> **Authorization**: [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens) with at least [Engineer](/guides/account-info/user-access-and-control/configuring-user-roles-and-permissions\") permissions.
"

"Recreate a secret based on the secret's name.

The secret field must be Base64-encoded because a secret can contain binary data.

> **Authorization**: [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens) with at least [Engineer](/guides/account-info/user-access-and-control/configuring-user-roles-and-permissions\") permissions.

post.summary: Lint (validate) VCL using flags set for the service
post.description:
"Services may have flags set by a Fastly employee or by the purchase of products as addons to the service, which modify the way VCL is interpreted by that service.  This endpoint validates the submitted VCL in the context of the specified service.
> **Authorization**: [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens) with at least [Engineer](/guides/account-info/user-access-and-control/configuring-user-roles-and-permissions\") permissions.
"

post.summary: Lint (validate) VCL using a default set of flags
post.description:
"This endpoint validates the submitted VCL against a default set of enabled flags. Consider using the /service/{service_id}/lint operation to validate VCL in the context of a specific service.
> **Authorization**: [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens) with at least [Engineer](/guides/account-info/user-access-and-control/configuring-user-roles-and-permissions\") permissions.
"

get.description:
"Returns the relationship between a Backend and a Director. If the Backend has been associated with the Director, it returns a simple record indicating this. Otherwise, returns a 404.

"

post.description:
"Establishes a relationship between a Backend and a Director. The Backend is then considered a member of the Director and can be used to balance traffic onto.
> **Authorization**: [API token](/reference/api/auth-tokens) with at least [Engineer](/guides/account-info/user-access-and-control/configuring-user-roles-and-permissions\") permissions.
"

delete.description:
"Deletes the relationship between a Backend and a Director. The Backend is no longer considered a member of the Director and thus will not have traffic balanced onto it from this Director.
